
# Shipping container identification number: Remove 50-character limit

As a User, I want the shipping container identification number field to validate only against the correct 11‑character business format,
so that I do not receive incorrect or contradictory error messages related to 50‑character limits.

> Note: This does not apply to the Plane transport type, which continues to use the original behaviour.

## Steps to Reproduce:

- Create any document (CC, PS, or NMD)
- Navigate to the Transport type section and select any transport type other than **Plane**, Plane will continue to use the original behaviour
- Enter more than 50 characters in the Shipping container number field
- Click Save and continue


## Expected Result:

- Only the new 11-character regex validation should be applied
- The legacy 50-character length validation should be removed

## Actual Result:

- The system still enforces the 50-character validation rule, indicating that legacy validation logic has not been fully removed


## Acceptance Criteria

### Scenario 1 — Remove the 50‑character validation rule

- Given I am entering a shipping container identification number
- When I enter a value of any length
- Then the system must not apply any minimum or maximum character length validation
- And no error message related to character length is displayed

### Scenario 2 — Remove the 50‑character field‑level error message from the application

- Given I am on any transportation details page containing the shipping container identification number field
- When validation occurs
- Then the application must not display the error message:

> "Container identification number must not exceed 50 characters"

- This message must not appear in either:
	- Field-level validation
	- Error summary

### Scenario 3 — Excessively long inputs trigger the existing format validation error only

- Given I enter an invalid shipping container identification number of any length
- When I press Save and continue
- Then only the standard format validation error is shown:

> "Enter a shipping container number in the correct format. This must be 11 characters: 3 letters, then U, J, Z or R, then 7 numbers."

- And the error appears in the error summary box
- And the field is marked with the error

### Scenario 4 — The Transport details section remains marked as Incomplete when invalid input is provided

- Given I have entered an invalid shipping container identification number of any length
- When I press Save and continue
- Then the Transport details section in `/progress` is marked as Incomplete

### Scenario 5 — No value is saved when the format is invalid

- Given I enter an invalid shipping container identification number
- When I press Save and continue
- Then the entered value is not saved to the application
- And any previously saved valid value (if present) remains unchanged

### Scenario 6 — Valid container identification number is accepted

- Given I enter a valid shipping container identification number in the correct 11-character format
- When I press Save and continue
- Then no validation errors are displayed
- And the value is saved successfully
- And the Transport details section on `/progress` is marked as Complete


** Aditional notes**

- Add 90% test coverege for code that we add as appropriate
